Description: Icon-only buttons must expose an accessible name
Do: Add aria-label (or visually-hidden text) to icon-only buttons
Don't: Ship icon buttons with no accessible name

Description: Every input needs a programmatically associated label
Do: Use <label for> tied to the input id (or wrap the input)
Don't: Rely on placeholder text as the only label

Keywords: semantic html aria role button link
Description: Use native semantic elements before ARIA roles
Do: Use <button>/<a>/<nav>/<main>; add role only when no native element fits
Don't: Use <div onclick> for interactive controls

Description: Async status changes must be announced to screen readers
Do: Put status/toasts in an aria-live region
Don't: Update content silently with no announcement

Description: Decorative images/icons must be hidden from assistive tech
Do: Set aria-hidden="true" (or empty alt) on decorative graphics
Don't: Let the screen reader announce every decorative glyph

Keywords: keyboard tab focus operable no trap
Description: All interactive elements must be reachable and operable by keyboard
Do: Ensure logical tab order and full keyboard support; no traps
Don't: Build controls that only respond to mouse/touch

Description: Keyboard focus must be clearly visible
Do: Keep a visible :focus-visible ring (2–4px)
Don't: Remove outlines with outline:none and no replacement

Description: Interactive targets should be at least 44x44px
Do: Pad small controls so the hit area reaches ~44px
Don't: Ship tiny icon links with a small clickable area

Description: Adjacent click targets need enough spacing
Do: Keep at least 8px between adjacent interactive targets
Don't: Cram buttons/links together with no gap

Description: Never gate primary actions behind hover alone
Do: Trigger actions on click; treat hover as enhancement
Don't: Reveal the only action path on :hover (fails touch/keyboard)

Description: Browser back/forward must work and reflect app state
Do: Use real routes/History API and keep URLs shareable
Don't: Hijack or reset history so Back breaks
Code Example Bad: location.hash=''; // wipes history, Back exits app

Description: Modals need a close affordance, Esc, and focus management
Do: Trap focus, close on Esc + button, restore focus on close
Don't: Open a modal with no Esc, no focus trap, no visible close

Description: Returning to a view should restore scroll and filter state
Do: Persist filters in the URL; allow native scroll restoration
Don't: Reset scroll position and clear filters on every navigation
Code Example Bad: // filters kept only in memory, lost on back

Description: Show visible feedback for operations over ~300ms
Do: Use a skeleton/spinner and mark the region aria-busy
Don't: Freeze the UI with no loading indication

Description: Confirm successful actions with brief, announced feedback
Do: Show a toast/inline confirmation in an aria-live region
Don't: Complete actions silently

Description: Show errors next to the field and link them for a11y
Do: Render inline error and reference it via aria-describedby
Don't: Only turn the border red with no message
Code Example Good: Enter a valid email

Description: Validate on blur/submit, not on every keystroke
Do: Validate onblur and on submit; focus first invalid field
Don't: Validate on every keystroke causing flicker

Keywords: input type email tel number url
Description: Use semantic input types for keyboard + validation
Do: Use type=email/tel/number/url with inputmode as needed
Don't: Use type=text for everything

Description: Enable browser/password-manager autofill
Do: Set autocomplete tokens (email, name, current-password…)
Don't: Disable autocomplete on standard fields

Description: Let users reveal their password
Do: Provide a show/hide toggle that flips type
Don't: Force blind typing with no reveal option

Description: Virtualize lists of ~50+ items
Do: Render only visible rows (e.g. react-window / virtual scroller)
Don't: Render thousands of DOM nodes at once

Description: Serve right-sized, modern, lazy-loaded images
Do: Use responsive srcset/sizes, WebP/AVIF, loading=lazy, width/height
Don't: Ship one huge image and no dimensions (causes CLS)

Description: Debounce/throttle high-frequency handlers
Do: Wrap search/scroll/resize handlers in debounce/throttle
Don't: Run heavy work on every input/scroll event

Description: Warm up critical third-party origins early
Do: Use rel=preconnect/preload for critical fonts/APIs
Don't: Let critical origins connect late in the waterfall
Code Example Bad: <!-- no preconnect; font connects during render -->

Description: Micro-interactions should be 150–300ms with real easing
Do: Use ease-out to enter/ease-in to exit; transform + opacity only
Don't: Use long or linear transitions on core UI

Description: Honor the user's reduced-motion preference
Do: Reduce/disable non-essential motion in the media query
Don't: Run parallax/large motion regardless of preference

Description: Reserve infinite animation for loaders/live data
Do: Loop only where it conveys ongoing activity
Don't: Keep decorative elements looping forever

Description: Body text must be readable; base ~16px
Do: Use ~16px base and rem units for scalability
Don't: Set body text below 12px or lock px sizes

Description: Layout must survive 200% browser zoom / larger text
Do: Use rem/em and fluid layouts; test at 200% zoom
Don't: Use fixed px heights that clip text when scaled

Description: Content must not hide behind sticky headers
Do: Reserve space / use scroll-margin-top for anchored content
Don't: Let a sticky header cover the scrolled-to content
Code Example Bad: position:sticky;top:0 /* overlaps content on anchor jump */

Description: Ensure ≥4.5:1 contrast in both light and dark themes
Do: Use semantic color tokens per theme; test both
Don't: Reuse light-theme grays directly in dark mode

Description: Don't hide essential controls behind hover/gesture only
Do: Keep essential actions visible; hover is enhancement only
Don't: Reveal the only way to act on :hover
